key_topics: Multi-digit multiplication: up to four digits by two digits
Long division: two-digit divisors
Fractions: equivalent fractions, comparing unlike fractions using benchmarks
Adding and subtracting fractions with unlike denominators
Multiplying fractions by whole numbers
Decimals: tenths and hundredths, ordering, comparing
Adding and subtracting decimals
Prime and composite numbers, prime factorisation
Factors and multiples: GCF, LCM
Angles: measuring with a protractor
Lines: parallel, perpendicular, intersecting
Area and perimeter of complex shapes
Volume: cubic units
competences: Multiply four-digit numbers by two-digit numbers
Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators
Add and subtract decimals to the hundredths
Find prime factorisation and determine GCF and LCM
typical_mistakes: Partial product errors in long multiplication
Finding LCM incorrectly (using product instead of least multiple)
Decimal alignment errors in addition and subtraction

key_topics: Reading: novels, poetry, myths, historical fiction
Comprehension: summarising, comparing texts, evaluating evidence
Literary elements: plot, conflict, theme, character development
Informational text: text structure (chronological, compare-contrast, cause-effect)
Writing: multi-paragraph essays (5-paragraph structure)
Research writing: using multiple sources, note-taking, bibliography
Grammar: subject-verb agreement, pronoun case (I/me, who/whom)
Modifiers: adjectives and adverbs for precision
Vocabulary: context clues, reference materials, Greek and Latin roots
competences: Write a five-paragraph essay on a given topic
Summarise texts accurately without personal opinion
Analyse text structure and explain how it supports meaning
Use reference materials to find word meanings and spelling
typical_mistakes: Five-paragraph essays with weak topic sentences
Pronoun case errors (him and me vs. he and I)

key_topics: Medieval History: feudalism, the Middle Ages in Europe
The Crusades: causes, major figures, consequences
The Renaissance: key figures (Leonardo, Michelangelo, Raphael), ideas
The Reformation: Martin Luther, religious conflict in Europe
Age of Exploration: Columbus, Magellan, Vasco da Gama
American History: Colonial period — the 13 colonies
American Revolution: causes, key battles, Declaration of Independence
World Geography: Africa — physical features and countries
competences: Describe the feudal system and medieval society
Explain the significance of Renaissance artists and ideas
Trace the Age of Exploration routes on a map
Describe the causes and outcome of the American Revolution

key_topics: Human Body: digestive system — organs, enzymes, absorption
Circulatory system: heart, blood, arteries, veins, capillaries
Respiratory system: lungs, gas exchange
Nervous system: brain, spinal cord, sensory and motor neurons
Electricity and Magnetism: circuits, electromagnets
Sound and Light: wave properties, reflection, refraction
Earth's Resources: renewable and non-renewable
Conservation and environmental protection
competences: Trace the path of food through the digestive system
Describe blood circulation through the heart and body
Explain the difference between renewable and non-renewable resources
Build a simple circuit and explain how it works
typical_mistakes: Thinking respiration and breathing are the same
